    "R1.1": "The artifact operationalizes 'predictive skill' as inter-model agreement rather than accuracy against observations — a conceptual conflation that must be reconstructed and assessed regardless of which checking mode is intended.",
    "R1.2": "Load-bearing under a scientific-validity read: the inference chain breaks at multiple points (agreement ≠ skill; two low-agreement regions are excluded post-hoc yet the headline claims 'all six regions'; the discussion equates small spread with reliable forecasting without independent validation). These are logical-consistency failures, not stylistic ones.",
    "R1.3": "Load-bearing under a scientific-validity read: the headline claim ('high predictive skill across all six regions') is directly contradicted by the data presented (NE Brazil skill 0.41, SE Australia 0.48 with sign disagreement), and the selective exclusion of those two regions from summary statistics without pre-registration inflates the reported mean from ~0.66 to 0.78."
  },

rationale.R1.1: The artifact operationalizes 'predictive skill' as inter-model agreement rather than accuracy against observations — a conceptual conflation that must be reconstructed and assessed regardless of which checking mode is intended.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing under a scientific-validity read: the inference chain breaks at multiple points (agreement ≠ skill; two low-agreement regions are excluded post-hoc yet the headline claims 'all six regions'; the discussion equates small spread with reliable forecasting without independent validation). These are logical-consistency failures, not stylistic ones.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing under a scientific-validity read: the headline claim ('high predictive skill across all six regions') is directly contradicted by the data presented (NE Brazil skill 0.41, SE Australia 0.48 with sign disagreement), and the selective exclusion of those two regions from summary statistics without pre-registration inflates the reported mean from ~0.66 to 0.78.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I check the scientific and logical validity of the claims and methodology (are the conclusions actually supported by the analysis?), or check the writing quality and clarity (grammar, phrasing, readability)?
